name: Yuanshi Tianzun
shortDescription: Yuanshi Tianzun is one of the highest deities in Taoism, often considered the First of the Three Pure Ones. He represents the origin of all things and the ultimate source of Taoist principles.
description: Yuanshi Tianzun, meaning "Celestial Venerable of the Primordial Beginning," is a central figure in Taoist cosmology and theology. He is regarded as the first of the Three Pure Ones (Sanqing), the highest deities in the Taoist pantheon. Yuanshi Tianzun is believed to predate the universe itself, existing in a state of Wuji (primordial chaos) before the differentiation of Yin and Yang. He is considered the origin of all things, the source from which the Tao itself emanates. Unlike some deities who actively intervene in human affairs, Yuanshi Tianzun is more of an abstract principle and a cosmic force, representing the ultimate reality. He is often depicted as an elderly man with a serene expression, holding a pearl symbolizing the potential of creation. His role is less about direct interaction and more about the fundamental principles that govern existence. He is believed to have created the universe and established the natural order. Some Taoist schools consider Laozi to be an incarnation of Yuanshi Tianzun, while others view them as distinct but related figures. Yuanshi Tianzun's significance lies in his representation of the ultimate source and the underlying principles of Taoism, embodying the concept of the Tao itself.
epithets: Celestial Venerable of the Primordial Beginning
